response:
**Results**

We evaluated ten experimental variants of our proposed method on the validation set, reporting mean accuracy and standard deviation across five random seeds for each configuration. Table 1 summarizes the validation accuracy (\%) for all variants. Variant_03, which omits the proposed component and thus serves as the baseline, achieved a mean accuracy of 67.8\% (±0.6). Among the nine variants incorporating different configurations of the proposed method, only variant_08 outperformed the baseline, attaining a mean accuracy of 70.4\% (±0.5). This represents an absolute improvement of +2.6 percentage points over the baseline.

The remaining variants did not surpass the baseline performance. Specifically, variant_01 achieved 67.2\% (±0.8), variant_02 reached 66.9\% (±0.7), and variant_05 attained 66.3\% (±1.0). Variants_09 and _10 achieved 66.5\% (±0.7) and 65.9\% (±0.8), respectively. Lower performances were observed for variant_04 (65.1\% ±0.9), variant_06 (64.7\% ±0.7), and variant_07 (65.8\% ±0.8). These results indicate that most configurations of the proposed component did not yield consistent improvements over the baseline.

In summary, only variant_08 demonstrated a clear and reproducible accuracy gain relative to the baseline, while the other eight configurations underperformed. This suggests that the effectiveness of the proposed method is highly sensitive to its configuration; careful selection and tuning are essential to realize its potential benefits. Further analysis of variant_08 may yield insights into the critical factors driving the observed improvement.
